mission.title: buildpacks: Use fully qualified image names (podman compatibility)
mission.description: Use fully qualified image names (podman compatibility). Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current buildpacks setup
steps.description:
Verify your buildpacks version and configuration:
```bash
pack version
```
This feature requires a working buildpacks installation.

steps.title: Review buildpacks configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant buildpacks configuration:
For podman compatibility (and more) it would be good to use fully qualified image names ( = include registry in image name)

Example: Instead of:

```
buildpacksio/lifecycle:0.11.3
```

use:
```
docker.io/buildpacksio/lifecycle:0.11.3
```

steps.title: Apply the fix for Use fully qualified image names (podman compatibility)
steps.description:
I like this suggestion although I know there will be some necessary discussion about ease-of-use considering that there's a large number of users that are coming from the docker CLI where the host is assumed if missing.
```yaml
buildpacksio/lifecycle:0.11.3
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Use fully qualified image names (podman compatibility)" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I like this suggestion although I know there will be some necessary discussion about ease-of-use considering that there's a large number of users that are coming from the docker CLI where the host is assumed if missing.

prerequisites.description: A working buildpacks installation or development environment.
